title: Cluster Networking, CNI, NetworkPolicy, Dual Stack, and Layered Troubleshooting · Stakeholder Platform Mission
summary: A public-interest data platform. The platform needs Service discovery, explicit NetworkPolicy, Gateway API routing and TLS, cross-namespace delegation, least exposure, status-driven diagnosis, and conformance limits. Planned artifact: Deliver Zero-Trust Service and Gateway Boundary with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace Service selectors, EndpointSlices, readiness and serving conditions, manual endpoints, topology hints, and stale endpoint diagnosis.
Build default-deny baselines while explicitly allowing DNS, required dependencies, control paths, metadata restrictions, and tested egress destinations.
Design Gateway addresses and listeners with protocol, port, hostname, namespace attachment, certificate reference, TLS mode, and conflict behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Project K8s Service Selector Endpointslices
steps.instruction:
project case — A project Pod can resolve DNS but cannot reach a backend after default deny; the policy selects only ingress, misses egress DNS, and assumes NetworkPolicy implementation behavior. Trace source, destination, port, direction, selector, CNI, and node boundary. Competency probe: Trace Service selectors, EndpointSlices, readiness and serving conditions, manual endpoints, topology hints, and stale endpoint diagnosis.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Project K8s Referencegrant Status Conformance
steps.instruction:
Append the Kubernetes evidence item headed "  # Evidence: k8s-referencegrant-status-conformance-25t3vl". Tailor its gateway-route.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic API or manifest review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run kubectl, Helm, kubeadm, and cluster commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Project K8s Referencegrant Status Conformance.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns K8s Referencegrant Status Conformance into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Project K8s Referencegrant Status Conformance, keep K8s Referencegrant Status Conformance connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-referencegrant-status-conformance-25t3vl
  - competency: k8s-referencegrant-status-conformance
    artifact: gateway-route.yaml
    decision: referencegrant status conformance
    environment: three-node dual-stack staging cluster across two zones
    fault-injection: withdraw the selected image digest after one node pulls it
    retained-proof: object UID generation observedGeneration and status conditions
    case-key: 25t3vl
    change: |
      gatewayClassName: conformant-public
      listener: https-status-example-test
      tls-certificate: platform/status-cert
      route: intake/status-route
      backend: intake/status-api:8080
      referenceGrant: platform-to-intake
      status: Accepted-Programmed-ResolvedRefs
    verify: kubectl get gateway,httproute,referencegrant -A -o json
    failure: Reject controller-free Gateway assumptions, cross-namespace references without grants, rule-order myths, stale status, or unverified TLS ownership.
    repair: Select a conformant implementation, bind listeners and Routes, authorize references, inspect observedGeneration conditions, and transfer-test traffic.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Authorize cross-namespace references with ReferenceGrant and verify Accepted, Programmed, ResolvedRefs, observedGeneration, attached routes, and implementation conformance.

hints: For Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project K8s Referencegrant Status Conformance, name the context, namespace, API identity, desired state, observed status, controller, policy, and failure-domain boundary, K8s Referencegrant Status Conformance precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project K8s Referencegrant Status Conformance, trace this concrete input and predict admission, defaulting, scheduling, reconciliation, status, traffic, storage, policy, disruption, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A project Pod can resolve DNS but cannot reach a backend after default deny; the policy selects only ingress, misses egress DNS, and assumes NetworkPolicy implementation behavior. Trace source, destination, port, direction, selector, CNI, and node boundary. Competency probe: Authorize cross-namespace references with ReferenceGrant and verify Accepted, Programmed, ResolvedRefs, observedGeneration, attached routes, and implementation conformance.

For Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project K8s Referencegrant Status Conformance, challenge the documented misconception with object, condition, event, policy, failure, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: A syntactically valid HTTPRoute is active even when status conditions reject its references.
